actionUnits.condition: Scene mapping confirmation and auto-ignite wait: This GameObject has a BoxCollider (enabled, non-trigger) and no Rigidbody. The attached MonoBehaviour component (m_Script guid: 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d; fields match: timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, totalHitTime=5, mainEffectGo fileID=1849464941111748095, onNeighborsThreshold=2) is the Fire script provided. No m_Calls (UnityEvent) are configured on this object. During this wait, Update() can auto-run and, if onNeighbors >= onNeighborsThreshold is satisfied by scene neighbors, CheckNeighborState() will set shouldTurnOn and the timer will elapse to isOn=true, calling UpdateFireState(true) which plays the particle system and notifies neighbors and FireController.

Extinguish sequence when isOn is true: This step assumes an external water projectile/system will contact this object's BoxCollider and invoke IWaterInteractable.WaterHit(Vector3) repeatedly within maxHitInterval=2s to accumulate currentHitTime >= totalHitTime=5s. That path exercises isTurningOff logic in Update(), transitions fire off, and calls UpdateFireState(true). No m_Calls are defined, and Fire.WaterHit takes a parameter, so we cannot directly invoke it via a no-arg Trigger; collisions must occur via gameplay.
